svg.title: Self-supervised paired ADC and T2 image synthesis architecture
svg.desc: Two paths: a generation path where random noise feeds shared layers driving GeneratorADC and GeneratorT2 to produce spatially aligned synthetic ADC and T2 images, and a reconstruction path where a real ADC image is encoded to a latent vector then decoded back into a reconstructed ADC and a synthesized T2, supervised by L1 loss against real paired images.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why sample noise from a standard normal distribution?')
sendPrompt('What are the shared layers and how do they guarantee alignment?')
sendPrompt('What is a synthetic ADC image?')
sendPrompt('What is a synthetic T2 image?')

svg.text: Shared weights make the two outputs spatially aligned
Path B — reconstruction from a real ADC

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the real paired ADC input?')
sendPrompt('What does the encoder learn to compress?')
sendPrompt('What is z_encoded and how does it relate to the noise z?')
sendPrompt('How is the same GeneratorADC reused on the encoded latent?')
sendPrompt('How is the same GeneratorT2 reused on the encoded latent?')
sendPrompt('What is the reconstructed ADC compared against?')
sendPrompt('What is the synthesized T2 compared against?')
sendPrompt('How does the L1 loss against real paired images train this network?')
g.text: L1 loss vs real paired images
